channel.title: Experimental Tools on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink
channel.description: Recent content in Experimental Tools on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink

item.title: Motor Control DIM Connection Viewer
item.description:
Interactive browser of every signal that crosses the 120-pin MCS DIM connector between a Motor Control DIM card (the MCU board) and a motor-control baseboard.

Open the viewer

🖥️ Open in full window

How to read the grid

Element Meaning
Left column DIM connector pin number (1 – 120)
Centre column Baseboard signal on that DIM pin, colour-coded by category (PWM, ANA, QEI, HAL, FLT, DBG, PWR, XPR, COM, PFC, NC)
Right column MCU pin name(s) — if multiple are listed, the first is the primary (directly connected) route; any below are alternates (via 0 Ω resistors) or DNP (see tooltip)

Category colour legend

  • 🟢 PWM — PWM outputs to the inverter / brake / PFC
  • 🟠 ANA — Analog input (current shunt, voltage divider, thermistor, potentiometer)
  • 🔵 QEI — Quadrature encoder input
  • 🟣 HAL — Hall-sensor input
  • 🔴 FLT — Fault / latch inputs
  • ⚫ DBG — Debug (PGD/PGC/MCLR, TX/RX)
  • ⚪ PWR / NC — VCC/VSS rails and no-connects
  • 🟡 PFC — PFC-specific (MCHV baseboard only)
  • 🟢 COM — On-board communication (EEPROM chip-select, etc.)
  • 🟣 XPR — Xplained Pro expansion header pass-through

Useful interactions

  • Hover any MCU-pin cell to see the full pin signature (all PPS / AD / CVD / CMP tags from the datasheet).
  • Click a DIM header to jump to its Confluence info sheet (when a link is available).
  • Toggle “Show alternates” to reveal secondary routing paths — usually DNP 0 Ω resistors reserved for hardware rework.
  • Toggle “Motor-control essentials” to dim everything that isn’t on the PWM / current / voltage / Hall / QEI / fault signal path — helpful when sanity-checking a control-loop wiring.

Related resources

  • mchp-board-dim-mapping skill — full methodology for maintaining the spreadsheet and adding new DIMs. (In-repo at .pi/skills/mchp-board-dim-mapping/SKILL.md; not served by Hugo.)
  • Motor Control Guide — high-level overview of the MCHP motor-control ecosystem.
  • Block Reference → PWM HS FEP — the PWM block that drives the inverter pins visible in this viewer.
  • Microchip Motor Control landing page — product-level information and development kits.

Data-source details

The embedded data is assembled from four authoritative inputs:
